description_long: This object is a solid cross-shaped grave marker, likely made from stone or concrete, with a simple and robust design. The surface appears rough and slightly irregular, suggesting it may have been weathered or hand-carved. The cross has thick, blocky arms and a sturdy vertical post, with no visible inscriptions or decorative elements. The base of the cross is uneven, possibly indicating it was once embedded in the ground. The color is a uniform pale white or light gray, typical of aged stone or concrete. This type of grave marker is commonly found in cemeteries and serves as a memorial for the deceased.
description: A rough, solid stone or concrete cross grave marker with a simple, blocky design and no visible inscriptions.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cross shape with thick arms and a slightly uneven base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and thickness of the cross, with visible roughness on the surface.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cross, similar in texture and form to the front, with a slightly more irregular surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cross's thickness and the rough, unfinished texture along the edge.
